rule: The "preceding 15-month period" begins on the same calendar day 15 months prior to the date of the violation being assessed and ends on the day before that violation occurred. If the starting calendar day does not exist, the period begins on the last day of that month.
The term "operator" refers to the legal entity that owned or operated the mine at the time the violation occurred.

An operator's history of previous violations is based on both the total number of violations and the number of repeat violations of the same citable provision (defined as the specific subsection of a standard) that reached finality under Rule 2 within the preceding 15-month period. Each distinct infraction counts as a separate violation toward the total.

Only assessed violations that have been paid in full (cleared funds), finally adjudicated (where both liability and penalty are determined and no further appeals are pending or can be filed), or have become final orders of the Commission (including default orders not currently set aside) are included in determining an operator's history.
